correctness.notes: Theorem 1 / sharpness and endpoint-attainment step: The proof uses missing witnesses gamma_x^U in argmax_gamma integral h_x d gamma and measurable selectors x -> gamma_x^U with integral h_x d gamma_x^U = U_x(P). From the definitions h_x(u,v)=1{Q_1(u|x)>Q_0(v|x)} is a strict-indicator Borel/lower-semicontinuous kernel, not a weakly continuous or upper-semicontinuous objective; compactness of Gamma_x does not reproduce upper endpoint attainment. The same issue affects the claimed closed conditional value set I_x=[L_x(P),U_x(P)].; Theorem 2 / root-n sample expansion for \label{thm:inference}: Dropped local sieve remainder S_n = sqrt(n){Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n)-Phi_rho(hat eta_n)}. Equivalently, after subtracting the pointwise bias, the missing term is sqrt(n){[Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n)-Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta(P))]-[Phi_rho(hat eta_n)-Phi_rho(eta(P))]}. The note controls only b_{K_n}=||Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta(P))-Phi_rho(eta(P))||=o(n^{-1/2}) and a bootstrap-scale local bound b_{K_n}^{loc}=o(m_n^{-1/2}); since m_n/n -> 0, sqrt(n)b_{K_n}^{loc} need not vanish.; Supporting theorem \label{thm:critical-set} / converse sign-undetermined claim: The converse from L_R(P) <= 1/2 <= U_R(P) drops the feasibility witnesses needed to make the sign genuinely data-undetermined: it does not construct feasible copula families gamma^- and gamma^+ with theta(gamma^-) <= 1/2 <= theta(gamma^+), nor a selector attaining theta=1/2. Straddling the outer envelope is only an outer-bound statement unless the sharpness/attainment result is imported.

perItemFindings.label: k-sharp-dual-confirmed
perItemFindings.one_line: [blocking] Theorem 1 / sharpness and endpoint-attainment step: The proof uses missing witnesses gamma_x^U in argmax_gamma integral h_x d gamma and measurable selectors x -> gamma_x^U with integral h_x d gamma_x^U = U_x(P). From the definitions h_x(u,v)=1{Q_1(u|x)>Q_0(v|x)} is a strict-indicator Borel/lower-semicontinuous kernel, not a weakly continuous or upper-semicontinuous objective; compactness of Gamma_x does not reproduce upper endpoint attainment. The same issue affects the claimed closed conditional value set I_x=[L_x(P),U_x(P)]. → The derivation gives an outer bound and at most a closure/approximation claim unless endpoint attainment and measurable selectors are added or proved. The claimed sharp closed identified interval [L_rho(P),U_rho(P)] is not established under Assumptions sampling--band.

perItemFindings.label: inference
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 2 / root-n sample expansion for \label{thm:inference}: Dropped local sieve remainder S_n = sqrt(n){Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n)-Phi_rho(hat eta_n)}. Equivalently, after subtracting the pointwise bias, the missing term is sqrt(n){[Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n)-Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta(P))]-[Phi_rho(hat eta_n)-Phi_rho(eta(P))]}. The note controls only b_{K_n}=||Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta(P))-Phi_rho(eta(P))||=o(n^{-1/2}) and a bootstrap-scale local bound b_{K_n}^{loc}=o(m_n^{-1/2}); since m_n/n -> 0, sqrt(n)b_{K_n}^{loc} need not vanish. → The claimed limit Z_n => dot Phi_{rho,P}(G_P) can be changed by the omitted local checkerboard error. The theorem needs a root-n local sieve-remainder condition, for example sup over an n^{-1/2}-neighborhood of eta(P) of ||Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta)-Phi_rho(eta)||=o(n^{-1/2}), or an equivalent along-sample condition.

perItemFindings.label: correctness
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Supporting theorem \label{thm:critical-set} / converse sign-undetermined claim: The converse from L_R(P) <= 1/2 <= U_R(P) drops the feasibility witnesses needed to make the sign genuinely data-undetermined: it does not construct feasible copula families gamma^- and gamma^+ with theta(gamma^-) <= 1/2 <= theta(gamma^+), nor a selector attaining theta=1/2. Straddling the outer envelope is only an outer-bound statement unless the sharpness/attainment result is imported. → As a front-matter theorem under Assumptions sampling--band, the iff claim is stronger than what the definitions imply. It must either be conditioned on the sharpness theorem or weakened to an outer-envelope diagnostic.

verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[blocking, conjecture:k-sharp-dual-confirmed] Theorem 1 / sharpness and endpoint-attainment step: The proof uses missing witnesses gamma_x^U in argmax_gamma integral h_x d gamma and measurable selectors x -> gamma_x^U with integral h_x d gamma_x^U = U_x(P). From the definitions h_x(u,v)=1{Q_1(u|x)>Q_0(v|x)} is a strict-indicator Borel/lower-semicontinuous kernel, not a weakly continuous or upper-semicontinuous objective; compactness of Gamma_x does not reproduce upper endpoint attainment. The same issue affects the claimed closed conditional value set I_x=[L_x(P),U_x(P)].. Fix: The derivation gives an outer bound and at most a closure/approximation claim unless endpoint attainment and measurable selectors are added or proved. The claimed sharp closed identified interval [L_rho(P),U_rho(P)] is not established under Assumptions sampling--band.
[serious, conjecture:inference] Theorem 2 / root-n sample expansion for \label{thm:inference}: Dropped local sieve remainder S_n = sqrt(n){Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n)-Phi_rho(hat eta_n)}. Equivalently, after subtracting the pointwise bias, the missing term is sqrt(n){[Phi_{rho,K_n}(hat eta_n)-Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta(P))]-[Phi_rho(hat eta_n)-Phi_rho(eta(P))]}. The note controls only b_{K_n}=||Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta(P))-Phi_rho(eta(P))||=o(n^{-1/2}) and a bootstrap-scale local bound b_{K_n}^{loc}=o(m_n^{-1/2}); since m_n/n -> 0, sqrt(n)b_{K_n}^{loc} need not vanish.. Fix: The claimed limit Z_n => dot Phi_{rho,P}(G_P) can be changed by the omitted local checkerboard error. The theorem needs a root-n local sieve-remainder condition, for example sup over an n^{-1/2}-neighborhood of eta(P) of ||Phi_{rho,K_n}(eta)-Phi_rho(eta)||=o(n^{-1/2}), or an equivalent along-sample condition.
[serious, setup] Supporting theorem \label{thm:critical-set} / converse sign-undetermined claim: The converse from L_R(P) <= 1/2 <= U_R(P) drops the feasibility witnesses needed to make the sign genuinely data-undetermined: it does not construct feasible copula families gamma^- and gamma^+ with theta(gamma^-) <= 1/2 <= theta(gamma^+), nor a selector attaining theta=1/2. Straddling the outer envelope is only an outer-bound statement unless the sharpness/attainment result is imported.. Fix: As a front-matter theorem under Assumptions sampling--band, the iff claim is stronger than what the definitions imply. It must either be conditioned on the sharpness theorem or weakened to an outer-envelope diagnostic.

Referee notes: I reproduced the sharpness/dual step for Theorem 1, the root-n and bootstrap decompositions for Theorem 2, and the downstream majority-sign use. The dual algebra itself matches, but the sharp endpoint-attainment step and the root-n sieve expansion both drop load-bearing conditions/terms.
